title: Cynometra minor
intro: Cynometra minor is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the minor cannonball tree. Traditionally, Cynometra minor has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cynometra minor include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cynometra minor is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or tablet, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Cynometra minor is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its leaf and flower structure. The genus Cynometra is a part of the tribe Detarieae, which includes over 40 species of trees and shrubs. Cynometra minor is a species within this genus, known for its unique characteristics and distribution. Further classification may include its order and family within the plant kingdom.
